title: pinus ponderosa subsp. benthamiana
intro: Pinus ponderosa subsp. benthamiana is a subspecies of the ponderosa pine tree, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its smaller leaves and distinctive cone shape. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Pinus ponderosa subsp. benthamiana are said to include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as potential protection against cancer and cardiovascular disease. Herbal preparations of Pinus ponderosa subsp. benthamiana typically involve the use of its needles or resin, which can be made into teas, tinctures, or infused oils for medicinal use.
